target.name: NetworkOcean
target.founder: Sam Mendel (CEO; serial founder, prior exit of a creator-hiring marketplace), Eric Kim (CTO; ex-Palantir, Merge; Cornell)

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $1M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
NetworkOcean is a seed-stage ocean and maritime technology company founded by serial entrepreneur Sam Mendel and ex-Palantir engineer Eric Kim. The company has raised $1M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of three private-stage peers in the ocean and maritime sector—Standard Subsea, Clippership, and Albacore—given the absence of direct public comparables in this frontier vertical.

A single triangulation method was applied: stage-progression analysis. Using the $1M total raised as a valuation proxy, a standard seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $2.5M. This methodology reflects typical venture return expectations at the seed-to-Series A progression point, absent deeper financial or market data specific to the target.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.0M (low) to $3.0M (high), with a median point of $2.5M. Key uncertainties center on the limited comp dataset available in this emerging maritime tech sector, the absence of public market benchmarks, and lack of data on NetworkOcean's current burn rate, market traction, or competitive positioning relative to named peers. Early-stage maritime and ocean tech remains sparsely capitalized relative to other venture sectors, which constrains confidence in any single methodology. Additional diligence on product-market fit and founder execution track record would be necessary to narrow this range.
